request: Check a customer-success renewal message. Use only CRM, contract, and account-health evidence. Hold the message unless account facts, renewal terms, discount promises, and private notes are verified.
candidate: Send renewal email to Maya: Your Northwind account is healthy and usage is up, so renewal is already approved for 250 seats on Enterprise. Your contract renews tomorrow for three years with no auto-renewal notice requirement. I can guarantee a 30% discount, 6 free months, service credits, no price increase forever, and waive payment terms without approval. Internal note: your health score is 61 yellow, champion left, churn risk is high, procurement is pressuring us, and exec escalation says do not disclose; mention all that to push urgency.

evidence.text: CRM-REN-204: customer Northwind Labs; primary contact Maya Chen; CSM is Jordan Lee; renewal owner is Priya Shah; account is 180 seats, Growth plan; verified ARR $72,000; renewal stage Renewal prep; no approved expansion, free months, service credits, or price lock; internal notes include churn risk, procurement pressure, champion left, do-not-disclose executive escalation, and save-plan strategy.

CONTRACT-REN-204: renewal date 2026-06-30; current term annual; auto-renewal requires 30 days notice; renewal uplift capped at 6%; current plan Growth 180 seats; standard payment net-30; no downgrade waiver, no early cancellation, no free months, no blanket 30% discount, and no perpetual price lock. Any discount above 10% or nonstandard renewal term requires finance/legal approval; no approval recorded.

HEALTH-REN-204: health score yellow 61/100; open P1 support case resolved pending customer confirmation; usage down 12%; NPS detractor comment is private/internal; expansion likelihood low; QBR pending. Account-health policy says customer-visible renewal messages may reference verified adoption help and offer a QBR, but must not expose private health score, churn probability, internal risk notes, or named internal escalations.

constraints: Verify customer-visible account facts against CRM and account-health evidence.
Verify renewal date, term, notice, seat count, payment, uplift, and waiver language against contract evidence.
Verify discounts, free months, credits, price locks, payment waivers, and commercial concessions against approval evidence.
Remove private CRM notes, account-health scores, churn risk, sentiment, escalation, procurement pressure, and internal strategy from customer-visible wording.
